name: BEN JOHN VILLABESA
title: Full-Stack Software Engineer // Building the Future

bio: Software engineer with 5+ years of experience building high-performance systems at scale. Passionate about clean architecture, distributed computing, and pushing the boundaries of what's possible on the web.
Currently focused on real-time data infrastructure and developer tooling. When not writing code, you'll find me contributing to open-source projects or reverse-engineering interesting systems.
I believe in writing code that's not just functional, but elegant — systems that are maintainable, performant, and a joy to work with.

projects.title: NEURAL NETWORK VISUALIZER
projects.description: Real-time 3D visualization of neural network architectures. Drag-and-drop layer configuration with live inference previews and gradient flow animation.
longDescription: Built a comprehensive web-based tool for visualizing neural network architectures in real-time 3D. Users can drag and drop layers to construct models, observe live inference as data flows through the network, and watch gradient flow animations during backpropagation.

The frontend renders complex graph structures using Three.js with custom WebGL shaders for data flow visualization. The backend runs actual PyTorch models through a FastAPI server, streaming intermediate activations via WebSockets.

Key technical challenge was maintaining 60fps rendering while continuously updating node activations from the inference server. Solved this with a double-buffered state system and GPU-accelerated particle effects for data flow.

projects.title: DECENTRALIZED CHAT PROTOCOL
projects.description: End-to-end encrypted peer-to-peer messaging built on WebRTC with distributed hash table routing. Zero server dependency after initial handshake.
longDescription: Designed and implemented a fully decentralized messaging protocol that operates without any central server after the initial peer discovery phase. Messages are end-to-end encrypted using the Signal Protocol adapted for a P2P context.

The routing layer uses a Kademlia-based DHT for peer discovery and message relay. NAT traversal is handled through a combination of STUN/TURN and hole punching techniques, achieving direct connections in 85% of cases.

Built the core networking layer in Rust compiled to WebAssembly for browser compatibility, with a React frontend that provides a familiar chat interface while the complexity of the P2P network is abstracted away.
features: Offline message queuing via relay nodes

projects.title: AUTONOMOUS DRONE SWARM
projects.description: Multi-agent coordination system for drone swarms using reinforcement learning. Collision avoidance and formation control in simulated environments.
longDescription: Developed a multi-agent reinforcement learning system that enables swarms of 50+ drones to coordinate autonomously. The agents learn cooperative behaviors like formation flying, area coverage, and target tracking without explicit programming.

The RL training pipeline uses a custom multi-agent PPO implementation with shared critic networks. Training runs on a cluster of 8 GPUs with the Gazebo simulator providing realistic physics and sensor models.
Achieved 95% collision avoidance rate in dense swarm scenarios and demonstrated emergent cooperative behaviors like leader-follower formation and dynamic task allocation.

projects.title: QUANTUM COMPILER TOOLKIT
projects.description: Transpiler and optimizer for quantum circuits targeting multiple hardware backends. Includes gate decomposition and noise-aware routing.
longDescription: Created an open-source quantum circuit compiler that takes high-level quantum algorithms and optimizes them for specific hardware backends including IBM, Google, and IonQ quantum processors.

The compiler performs multi-pass optimization: first decomposing arbitrary gates into the hardware's native gate set, then routing qubits to match the physical connectivity graph, and finally applying noise-aware scheduling to minimize decoherence errors.

The core compiler is written in Rust for performance, with Python bindings for integration with Qiskit and Cirq. The LLVM-inspired intermediate representation allows for easy addition of new optimization passes.
